name: anime.js (Lightweight JavaScript Animation Library)

performance.notes: RAF-based animation; very low CPU overhead; hardware-accelerated via CSS transforms

agent_readiness.notes: Lightweight (~17KB) animation engine. anime({ targets, properties, duration, easing, delay, loop }). Animate CSS properties, SVG attributes, DOM attributes, JS objects. Staggering with anime.stagger(). Keyframes array. Timeline with anime.timeline(). Promise-based with .finished. Easing functions, spring physics. SVG path animation (path drawing, motion along path). v4 rewrites in TypeScript with improved API. Not for React (use Framer Motion); best for vanilla JS/jQuery-style DOM animation.

gotchas: v4 has breaking changes from v3 (API redesign)
Not React-friendly (use Framer Motion or Motion One instead)
best_when: Vanilla JS or jQuery-based projects needing complex animations
SVG animation and path drawing effects
Lightweight alternative to GSAP for simple-to-medium complexity
avoid_when: React/Vue/Svelte apps (use Framer Motion or Motion One)
summary: anime.js is a lightweight (~17KB) JavaScript animation library. Simple API: anime({ targets, properties, duration, easing }). Staggering, keyframes, timelines, SVG path animation. v4 TypeScript rewrite with improved API. Best for vanilla JS projects needing multi-element choreography or SVG animation. React apps should use Framer Motion or Motion One; complex scroll animations should use GSAP ScrollTrigger.
